practice: Set explicit task-level retries and exponential backoff for tasks with external dependencies, and alert on DAGs exceeding their historical p95 runtime rather than a single fixed timeout.

Monitor consumer lag trend, not just its instantaneous value; a slowly growing lag over several intervals indicates a consumer that cannot keep pace with partition throughput and needs additional consumer instances.
Investigate pod restart counts alongside resource requests/limits; frequent restarts with memory near the configured limit usually indicate the limit is undersized rather than an application defect.
Validate pipeline parameter and trigger configuration after any deployment, since malformed trigger definitions commonly manifest as missing or malformed run timestamps downstream.
Cross-check batch job scheduling windows against known peak-load periods; jobs queued behind higher-priority batch work will show elevated runtime without any underlying defect.
Correlate equipment event codes with maintenance history before escalating; a repeated event code shortly after scheduled maintenance often indicates an incomplete repair rather than a new fault.
Treat isolated sensor anomalies (temperature, battery, signal) as noise unless they persist across multiple consecutive readings from the same device.
